response:
```python
import pytest

from your_module import is_palindrome


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "value,expected",
    [
        ("racecar", True),
        ("RaceCar", True),
        ("never odd or even", True),
        ("hello", False),
        ("abcba", True),
        ("abca", False),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_happy_path(value, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(value) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "value,expected",
    [
        ("", True),              # empty string is equal to its reverse
        ("a", True),             # single character
        (" ", True),             # spaces are removed
        ("   ", True),           # only spaces
        ("A", True),             # single uppercase character
        ("Aa", True),            # case-insensitive
        ("a a", True),           # spaces ignored
        ("nurses run", True),    # common palindrome phrase
        ("No lemon no melon", True),
        ("abc cba", True),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_edge_cases(value, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(value) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "value,expected",
    [
        ("race a car", False),
        ("palindrome", False),
        ("ab", False),
        ("A man a plan a canal Panama!", False),  # punctuation is not removed
        ("123abccba321", True),
        ("123abccbx321", False),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_non_palindromes_and_special_cases(value, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(value) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize("value", [None, 123, ["racecar"], {"s": "racecar"}])
def test_is_palindrome_invalid_types_raise(value):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        is_palindrome(value)
```
